zh.title: 以太坊/美元技术分析：震荡偏多延续
zh.content:
当前ETH/USD整体维持震荡偏多结构，价格运行于1720附近并稳居20日均线上方，短线多头仍占优势，但上行动能略有放缓，进入高位整理阶段

MACD指标仍维持正向扩张，动能柱稳定但未出现明显放大，说明趋势延续但缺乏加速。若RSI中性偏高，将提示短线可能震荡整理

下方支撑关注1704及1715区间，上方阻力位于1728及1745附近。当前价格贴近阻力带，若突破需放量确认，否则易回踩支撑

交易策略建议以区间思路为主，回踩1704附近可分批布局多单，止损设于1710下方；若放量突破1728，可顺势追多，但需控制仓位防止假突破

en.title: Ethereum vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Ethereum (ETH/USD) is currently exhibiting a consolidation phase following a period of muted volatility, with price action hovering around 1720.95. The market remains slightly constructive as price holds above the 20-day SMA at 1704, suggesting a mild bullish bias. However, the 24-hour range is narrow, indicating equilibrium between buyers and sellers. Momentum is not strongly directional, and the market appears to be awaiting a catalyst for a breakout.

The MACD reading at 18.15 indicates that bullish momentum is still present, although the lack of a signal line reading limits full confirmation of trend strength. Price stability above the SMA(20) reinforces a short-term bullish structure. With RSI unavailable, momentum assessment relies more heavily on price action and MACD behavior. Overall, momentum appears neutral-to-slightly bullish but not yet strong enough to confirm a breakout trend continuation.

Immediate support is found at the intraday low near 1715.39, with stronger structural support aligning with the SMA(20) at 1704.01. A breakdown below this zone would weaken the bullish bias and expose lower liquidity levels. On the upside, immediate resistance is located at 1728.44, the recent high. A decisive break above this level would be required to confirm renewed bullish continuation and expand upward momentum.

Trading strategy favors a range-bound approach with a slight bullish tilt. Long positions may be considered on dips toward 1715–1704 with tight risk management below the SMA support. Breakout traders should wait for a confirmed move above 1728.44 before targeting higher levels, potentially toward 1750. Until a clear breakout occurs, sideways accumulation remains the dominant scenario, requiring disciplined risk control and reduced position sizing.
